item.title: China’s Hot, Unprofitable AI Stocks Are Hard to Short Until July
item.description: Short sellers eying China’s hottest AI firms have found the going hard with so little of their shares publicly traded. That scarcity premium may come to an end in July as stock lockups expire.

item.title: AI Bond Binge Overwhelms Wall Street, Pushing Alphabet Overseas
item.description: Bankers were still putting the final touches on Alphabet Inc.’s blockbuster $17 billion of bond sales when word started to spread Monday morning on Wall Street: the company is already hawking more debt.

item.title: ASX Names Former Euronext Paris Chief Anthony Attia as CEO
item.description: ASX Ltd. named Anthony Attia its new chief executive, concluding a search after its former boss Helen Lofthouse said she was standing down earlier this year.

item.title: Gold Holds Decline as Rising US Inflation Raises Rate-Hike Bets
item.description: Gold held a decline as a resurgence in US inflation reinforced bets the Federal Reserve will keep interest rates higher for longer.

item.title: Oil Steady Ahead of Trump-Xi Talks as Iran War Tensions Simmer
item.description: Oil was steady ahead of a meeting between US President Donald Trump and his Chinese counterpart Xi Jinping, which is being held against the backdrop of the Iran war that shows no signs of a near-term resolution.

item.title: Asian Stocks to Climb as Wall Street Hits New High: Markets Wrap
item.description: Stocks in Asia were primed to rally Thursday after a US tech rally drove new highs on Wall Street, quelling concerns over inflation that have fueled wagers the Federal Reserve will keep rates higher for longer.

item.title: PEP Firms Sweeten Loan Terms as Volatility Hits Riskier Debt
item.description: Underwriters of two proposed loans to Australia-based Pacific Equity Partners’ companies have sweetened the terms, people familiar with the matter said, signaling investor demand for greater returns in a volatile market.

item.title: Air New Zealand Forecasts Full-Year Loss as Fuel Costs Surge
item.description: Air New Zealand expects a substantial full-year loss as the Middle East conflict stokes jet-fuel costs, forcing it to cut costs and reduce services.

item.title: Why the US Must Engage China on AI Safety Before It’s ‘Game Over’
item.description: Sebastian Mallaby of the Council on Foreign Relations says Chinese AI is closing the gap—and that means Washington can’t afford to ignore safety talks.

item.title: Cisco Gives Better-Than-Anticipated Forecast, Plans to Cut Jobs
item.description: Cisco Systems Inc. gave a fourth-quarter sales forecast that exceeded analysts’ estimates, signaling that the artificial intelligence build-out is fueling demand for networking gear.

item.title: AI Chipmaker Cerebras Expects to Price Its IPO at $185 Per Share
item.description: Cerebras Systems Inc. expects to price its US initial public offering at $185 per share, according to people familiar with the matter, as the artificial intelligence chipmaker seizes on the surging demand for semiconductors.

item.title: TPG Says New OpenAI Venture a Traditional Move for the PE Firm
item.description: OpenAI may be a marquee corporate name but working with the tech giant is business as usual for TPG Inc.

item.title: ECB’s Lagarde Sees Make-or-Break Moment to Reform European Union
item.description: European Union leaders must show courage in strengthening the bloc’s foundations, according to European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde.

item.title: Canadian Miner to Start Graphite Project in Challenge to China Dominance
item.description: Nouveau Monde Graphite Inc. expects to formally green-light plans this week to build one of North America’s few graphite projects, as countries seek to weaken China’s dominance over the critical mineral.

item.title: Musk’s xAI Races to Get Wall Street Firms to Use Grok Chatbot
item.description: Elon Musk’s xAI has recruited multiple Wall Street firms with ties to the billionaire’s business empire to test its Grok chatbot, according to people familiar with the matter, part of a push to bolster revenue ahead of parent company SpaceX’s initial public offering.

item.title: Judge Says Cannot ‘Rubber Stamp’ $1.5 Million Musk-SEC Deal
item.description: A federal judge cited “red flags” about a proposed $1.5 million deal between Elon Musk and the Securities and Exchange Commission to end the agency’s lawsuit alleging the world’s richest person waited too long in 2022 to reveal his growing stake in Twitter Inc.

item.title: Hackers Are Already Using AI to Beef Up Their Attacks, Hide Their Activity
item.description: Security personnel are catching attackers in new ways, too.

item.title: Venezuela Government Announces Debt Restructuring Process
item.description: The Venezuelan government announced the start of a “comprehensive and orderly” restructuring process for its sovereign debt and that of state-oil company PDVSA.

item.title: ECB’s Lane Outlines Factors That Will Affect Rate Move
item.description: European Central Bank Chief Economist Philip Lane kept his cards close to his chest on whether he’ll propose an interest-rate hike next month.

item.title: Varda Deepens Drugs-in-Space Bet After Win With AIDS Medication
item.description: Varda Space Industries Inc. is expanding plans for orbital pharmaceutical manufacturing beyond its recent success in crystallizing HIV drug Ritonavir in space, Chief Executive Officer Will Bruey said.

item.title: Silicon Valley Prosecutor Targets Foreign Corporate Spying
item.description: Silicon Valley’s top federal prosecutor said his top priorities include cases of trade-secret theft and economic espionage involving foreign adversaries such as China.

item.title: Brazil Real Falls on Report Bolsonaro Negotiated With Master CEO
item.description: Brazil’s currency slumped Wednesday after a news website linked right-wing presidential candidate Flavio Bolsonaro to Daniel Vorcaro, the former chief executive of a failed bank at the center of a massive fraud probe.

item.title: Not All Inflation Is Created Equal
item.description: Some particularly problematic price rises.

item.title: Odey Settles Sexual Assault Cases Ahead of London Trial
item.description: Hedge fund boss Crispin Odey has settled a handful of personal injury claims brought by women claiming sexual assault.

item.title: Bank of Canada Sees No Clear Rate Path Amid Tariff and Oil Risks
item.description: Bank of Canada officials discussed a “range of views” on the most likely path for interest rates last month, weighing various possible outcomes of the Iran war and a review of the North American trade agreement.

item.title: Hormuz Oil Flows Fell Nearly 30% Last Quarter, EIA Says
item.description: Flows of crude oil and fuels through the Strait of Hormuz fell by nearly 6 million barrels per day in the first quarter of 2026, the start of a seismic energy shock that has upended global supplies and sent prices surging.

item.title: Ford Shares Soar After Morgan Stanley’s Bullish Call on Energy
item.description: Ford Motor Co. shares surged Wednesday after a bullish call from Morgan Stanley on the automaker drew investor attention to the company’s energy storage business.

item.title: Treasury Buyers Get 5% Long-Bond Rate for First Time Since 2007
item.description: Investors snagged 5% yields on 30-year Treasuries for the first time since 2007, as surging energy prices push inflation — and expectations for more of it — higher.

item.title: Microsoft Spent Over $100 Billion on OpenAI Partnership
item.description: Microsoft Corp. has spent more than $100 billion on its partnership with OpenAI, a sum that underscores the significance of the software maker’s role in the AI company’s growth.

item.title: Geothermal Energy Firm Fervo Rises 33% After $1.89 Billion IPO
item.description: Fervo Energy Co. shares opened 33% above their IPO price after the geothermal energy developer raised $1.89 billion in an upsized US initial public offering.
